title: Adam the confusing toy
wikitext:
[]: Adam the confusing toy.             Quote Post by defiate » Wed Nov 09, 2005 5:14 pm So I stumbled upon this during my ventures on ebay: Anyone notice something peculiar? The toy for sale here is a Kubrick Adam (translated as the "Giant of Light" in the 2nd pic) but at the same time it is labelled as the "2nd angel" ... :shock: This couldn't have happened by error because Kubrick is a very respectable and sought after toy manufacturer and have partnered with Gainax to created these limited edition Kubrick Evangelion figures. There's no way Gainax or the company would have allowed such a mistake to pass through unless it was intentional... which leads me to my question: Is Adam (or the Giant of Light) considered the 2nd angel, as opposed to being the first as stated by many Evangelion neophytes, or is Adam not supposed to be numbered at all given the results of long ended debates about this? From what I understand Adam is the source of all angels and stands on the "fruit of life" side, whilst Lilith is the source of humans/lilim (then why are humans refered to as the 18th angel in EoE?). Wouldn't that make Adam "angel zero" instead of the 2nd? Is there something I have missed, or am I making a complete fool out of myself? (I still don't doubt the toy box... it has (C) Gainax clearly written there)[/img]

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Wed Nov 09, 2005 5:37 pm You've missed something very important. Adam and Lillith are indifferent, except in their forms. They are both sources of life, and may take away lives if ever combined with it's own clone, to start the Third Impact. But that's one big mistake. Unless Adam is really the 2nd Angel, but since there are so many Christianity related things, I doubt it was done intentionally. Since Adam was the first human.

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Wed Nov 09, 2005 5:46 pm Superdoughboy4: Adam and Lillith are indifferent, except in their forms. They are both sources of life, and may take away lives if ever combined with it's own clone, to start the Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Wed Nov 09, 2005 5:49 pm I was answering the question through the whole post. And I know what I was talking about. I meant that the Adam in the Bible, as in the first human, would connect to the Adam in Neon Genesis Evangelion, the first Angel. Not background story-wise though. That'd be much different.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Nov 09, 2005 6:06 pm Well, although Ritsuko referrs to Adam as the first angel in episode #15, it's not clear exactly which angel should be labeled as the first or second one. Either Adam or Lilith would be a candidate. the consensus seems to be that the angels were named in order of their discovery. So which was found first? Adam or Lilith?

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Wed Nov 09, 2005 6:28 pm Well assuming that the Black Moon (Geofront) and consequently Lilith was discovered after the second impact, and if angels are really named after order of discovery, then it would be safe to assume that Adam is the first angel. The problem is that there is no hard evidence in the series AFAIK that says that Lilith and the Black Moon was discovered after 2I. Also, would Lilith be classed as an angel? After all she didn't spawn the angels but the human race/lilim. It would be illogical to class her or us as angels (as stated in EoE, we are the 18th angel) it would be like saying humans are related to ducks and spawned from pterydactyls (those dinosaur birds)

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Wed Nov 09, 2005 6:35 pm ...? Adam is not the source of Angels. Lillith is not the source of Angels. They are both sources of life as in, human life. But that was manipulated through the process of cloning, which came out to be Ayanami and Kawouru-kun. Humans being the 18th Angel was a sort of metaphoric term. As in, the final stage of an Angel, which Kawouru was very close to. And, yeah. Angels are classified in order they are found. Gendou told the NERV control panel-ist to label Unit-04 as the # Angel. I forgot the number. Was it 13th?

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Wed Nov 09, 2005 6:49 pm Yeah you're right about the angel numbering system (finally i agree with you on something! :P) and Eva 03 was the 13th angel. But then I remember reading somewhere, most likely in these forums... the only eva forum i read (did you hear that Evamonkey?), that adam was the source of angels, which is why angels are constantly searching for Adam. In fact that's all they exist for- Finding Papa Adam. That is why when an angel comes into contact with Adam, the Third Impact would allegedly occur. I understand what you mean by Kaworu being close to human form and therefore humans being termed the 18th angel, but I still believe that someone out there has a very convincing argument that humans spawned from Lilith and therefore are called Lilim, and only human souls are collected by Lilith during the Instrumentality. That is also why Lilith was used to implement the ÃŽnstrumentality. (I don't know how to fit the Adam Embryo into this :oops: ) OMF do you mind helping us out here?

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Wed Nov 09, 2005 7:37 pm I just took what I read off of Eva Otaku, and implemented it into that post. Why wouldn't the Angels search for Lillith too? They are sorta Kazhah and Dozhah, in an asexual sense.

[]:             Quote Post by akda1ndaonly1 » Wed Nov 09, 2005 11:14 pm defiate wrote: Yeah you're right about the angel numbering system (finally i agree with you on something! :P) and Eva 03 was the 13th angel.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Nov 09, 2005 11:22 pm I always had the suspicion that Lilith wasn't in Evas plans until well into the series. And that its first apparition and naming being in #24 is more than a coincidence or plot twist. I believe that originally, at least until after Kaji names Lilith as Adam, that they only planned to have 1 SoL and the missing 2nd Angel would probably be humans themselves. But anyways, that toy could be wrong, t'could be just an error. Invocation of authority (because of the company's name) is a logical fallacy when trying to use it to base an argument. Other official and even canon material makes mistakes, why can't a kubrick set then?

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Fri Dec 30, 2005 9:21 pm Not to thread mine or anything, but I bought that set and it has issues with the characters eyes. Adam has white eyes instead of black....and Keel has eyes under his removable visor, and they are red.....

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Fri Dec 30, 2005 11:15 pm In #21', the White Moon is labeled GF1 (i.e Geofront 1). That tends to imply that it, and thus Adam, was discovered first. The Angels are numbered in order of their discovery or appearance, thus Adam = 1st, Lilith = 2nd, Sachiel = 3rd, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Sat Dec 31, 2005 1:05 pm Considering that Lilith "doesn't exist" until #24, if it really was a new plot twist done on the fly, could it be that the original plan for the 2nd angel was humanity, instead of being the 18th?

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sat Dec 31, 2005 5:06 pm I'm just forever going to be confused on the eye color issue, I also ordered the set with Kaworu at least he's supposed to have red eyes.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Jan 01, 2006 9:20 am AchtungAffen wrote: Considering that Lilith "doesn't exist" until #24, if it really was a new plot twist done on the fly, could it be that the original plan for the 2nd angel was humanity, instead of being the 18th?
